Now the first part of understanding how to sell online is knowing whether your product or service is actually ready to be sold online and one of the techniques I use is called the advertising core for it's going to be part of the BPM method and you're going to be able to go deeper into this to know how to score your product, how to see how strong your offer is and to be able to you know compare your audience build versus a really strong one and also your funnel your sales funnel or whatever funnel you've set up as well and you know based on years and years of doing this of auditing hundreds and hundreds if not even close to thousands of ad accounts since 2014 there's a pattern that we've noticed as to why some of the products don't sell, why some of the services are harder to find leads for or to push into a telephone call and it comes down to four simple principles and if those principles are not aligned that's where the struggle happens so the four principles are your product, your offer, your audience and your funnel so we start off with the product is it actually positioned well for the market does it clearly solve a problem or create a new better opportunity for customers now part of the second video here we're going to talk about Maslow's hierarchy where I will explain how that's constructed but this video is really just to prime you into really what's coming up in the BPM method and the whole BPM method program is split into these four areas because each and every one of these are so critical to get right but so powerful when they are set up properly so right now we're concerned about products how well is your product positioned in the marketplace this is about understanding whether we need to do more work in terms of explaining the product or service or if it's a well-known product or service we don't need to explain so much of can we get them you know can we get that click out of the ad platform wherever you're advertising and do it in a way that we pick out the best people so you know if you've seen the previous video earlier on in the BPM method we want to reach the right prospect not not everyone we don't want to maximize impressions or reach or you know just you know run broad ads to literally anyone and see what happens we want to reach the right people and part of that is in how you pitch the product or service to your prospect so just have that in mind as you go through the early part of the BPM method advertising isn't always hey you know you know I found this product or I've got this service or you know my client has this and I'm going to try and advertise it if that's your start point chances are you might fail if you know you've not run ads before and you're looking to get started but if you start with a mindset of will someone buy this product or will someone sign up for this service if so why who are they why would they be interested in our products or service how can I convince this person to click to hit my landing page to give me their email address so I can send them a load of emails or jump on the call that's where you need to start off with so I've seen many many products come and go you know for example let's take dropshipping over the many years it's been you know one of the processes to go off to somewhere like AliExpress find a product test it and see if it works but where do people stop and say is this product actually solving a problem is it useful do I already have an existing market that's one of the biggest things to look at is you know when you find a product or service is it a blue ocean is it a red ocean now a blue ocean is concerned with an opportunity with a low competition a red ocean is an opportunity with a lot of competition now say for example we are selling a service for a Facebook ads or meta ads agency are there many meta ads agencies on the planet probably now let's turn that and say how many meta ads agencies are there in Barbados I'm just using a random country just to make a make an extreme point probably not a lot now if there were many businesses in a country let's say Barbados they needed leads lead generation and things like that I don't know maybe there's one or two ads agencies maybe you can come in and pitch yourself as the number one place to go you build up your case studies you you pitch your product and service specifically tailored for that market now all of a sudden you're creating a blue ocean for yourself so you're not in these Facebook groups and forums and you know thinking to yourself oh there's so many ads agencies out there how am I going to compete you carve out a specific niche so even within it an existing product you know whether you're selling phone cases or you know beauty products or whatever it is you can still carve out your blue ocean from that and that's something which through the BPM method you'll learn about because once you've got a clear position for your products it makes building offers which are your ads which are the bridge between your product and your audience so much easier because when you understand what your product is serving people with building offers building ads and then building that into your funnel and finding the audiences everything else becomes a lot easier as well so I won't go into the detail of how to score this sheet that will come later on but I'm priming you ready to be thinking about your product first because it's it is honestly the most crucial part of getting your ads game working is to make sure you've positioned your product in the right
